properties.description: This initiative enforces security best practices for Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) clusters, including network configuration, authentication, and add-on policies. By implementing these policies, you can enhance the security posture of your AKS clusters and ensure compliance with organizational standards.

nonComplianceMessages.message: AKS clusters must adhere to security best practices for network configuration, authentication, identity, and add-on enablement. Review the AKS security best practices on TechDocs for remediation guidance.
AKS clusters must use Azure CNI with Overlay mode. This decouples pod IP allocation from the VNet address space, improving scalability and reducing IP exhaustion risk in enterprise environments.
nonComplianceMessages.policyDefinitionReferenceId: Enforce AKS Azure CNI with Overlay mode

nonComplianceMessages.message: AKS clusters must enable Azure RBAC for Kubernetes authorization. This integrates Kubernetes access control with Entra ID, providing centralized audit trails and consistent permission governance across Azure.
AKS clusters must enable the OIDC issuer. This is a prerequisite for Workload Identity, which allows pods to authenticate to Azure services using federated credentials without storing secrets.

AKS clusters must enable the Secrets Store CSI Driver add-on. This allows workloads to retrieve secrets directly from Azure Key Vault as volume mounts, eliminating hardcoded credentials and reducing secret sprawl.
nonComplianceMessages.policyDefinitionReferenceId: Enforce AKS Secrets Store CSI Driver

nonComplianceMessages.message: AKS clusters must enable Advanced Container Networking Services (ACNS) security features. ACNS provides eBPF-based L7 network policy enforcement and threat detection for container workloads.
AKS clusters must use the Cilium dataplane. Cilium provides eBPF-based networking with advanced network policy enforcement, observability, and security capabilities required for production workloads.
